basic_profile.legal_system: Civil law (Código Civil); tax law in the Estatuto Tributario

capital_controls.value: Operates an exchange-control regime (régimen cambiario); crypto is not a divisa (foreign currency) and cannot be channeled through the FX market, so crypto-funded inflows are not registrable

general_attitude_bitcoin.value: Legal to own and trade; not legal tender; regulators cautious; a comprehensive digital-assets law remains stalled in Congress

tax_residence_basis.value: Resident if present >183 days within any rolling 365-day window; residents taxed on worldwide income and assets

bitcoin_classification.value: DIAN treats cryptoassets as intangible assets forming part of patrimonio; declared at year-end value

capital_gains_treatment.value: Crypto gains may be treated differently depending on holding period, taxpayer profile, and whether the activity is habitual; local tax counsel should confirm the exact treatment
capital_gains_treatment.note: Human-reviewed 2026-06-16: no primary DIAN source for the exact >2yr/15% vs ordinary formulation for Bitcoin; softened.

wealth_tax.value: Colombia has a permanent individual wealth tax (impuesto al patrimonio, graduated 0.5-1.5%), generally keyed to UVT thresholds; use 72,000 UVT unless a newer primary source proves otherwise. A reported ~40,000 UVT figure is not used absent a current legislative source
wealth_tax.note: Human-reviewed 2026-06-16: cleared for existence + 72,000 UVT; 40,000 UVT figure dropped pending a primary source.

exit_tax.value: No deemed-disposal exit tax confirmed this pass; not affirmatively verified

legal_to_own.value: Yes; owning, buying, selling and trading crypto is permitted (not legal tender, not a regulated financial product)

exchanges_licensed.value: Per SFC Circular Externa 029/2014, virtual currencies are not regulated or supervised by the SFC; no exchange licensing regime; supervised entities are not authorized to custody, invest in, or intermediate them

self_custody_allowed.value: No prohibition on individual self-custody found; not affirmatively confirmed by a primary statement

crs_participation.value: Colombia is a committed CRS jurisdiction (first exchanges ~2017)

foreign_account_reporting.value: Tax residents with foreign assets > 2,000 UVT must file the Declaración de Activos en el Exterior (Form 160); foreign-held Bitcoin counts

crypto_declaration.value: DIAN Resolución 000240/2025 requires crypto-asset service providers to report user/transaction data from TY2026 (CARF-aligned)

forced_heirship.value: Forced heirship applies. Since Ley 1934 of 2018 the estate splits in two: the legítima rigurosa (half) is reserved for forced heirs (legitimaries), and the other half is now freely disposable by the testator (the former cuarta de mejoras and cuarta de libre disposición were merged into one freely-disposable quarter-mass). Older 50/25/25 descriptions are pre-2018
forced_heirship.note: Corrected + verified 2026-06-16 against Ley 1934/2018; prior profile text described the pre-2018 regime.

inheritance_tax.value: Inheritance/donation tax treatment (commonly described as ganancia ocasional) should be confirmed against DIAN/Congress primary text before relying on it
inheritance_tax.note: Human-reviewed 2026-06-16: primary DIAN/Congress source not retrieved; do not publish as confirmed.

trust_recognition.value: Foreign common-law trust recognition for estate planning not confirmed; Colombia uses domestic fiducia mercantil

marital_property_regime.value: Default regime is the sociedad conyugal (community of marital gains) absent a prenuptial agreement (capitulaciones); property acquired during the marriage is shared on dissolution, separate from the succession

crypto_banking_friction.value: Significant: banks have closed crypto-related accounts; SFC stance discourages servicing crypto firms; crypto-funded inflows cannot be channeled through the FX market

tax_residence_triggers.value: >183 days in any rolling 365-day window triggers worldwide-income residency; accidental-residence risk for long stays

moving_residence.value: Long stays can trigger worldwide-income tax residence; crypto-funded inflows face FX-registration friction; document source of funds early

direction_of_travel.label: reporting-heavy
direction_of_travel.rationale: CARF-aligned crypto reporting from TY2026, CRS participation, Form 160 foreign-asset declaration, beneficial-ownership registry
direction_of_travel.counterevidence: Enforcement capacity and exchange-data quality unproven

direction_of_travel.label: cross-border-fragile
direction_of_travel.rationale: Forced heirship + situs rules + unconfirmed trust recognition + FX-registration friction complicate cross-border Bitcoin inheritance and inflows
direction_of_travel.counterevidence: A pending digital-assets law could add clarity if passed

open_questions.question: Current-year wealth-tax threshold (72,000 UVT permanent law vs reported 2026 emergency-decree 40,000 UVT)
Whether any deemed-disposal/exit tax applies on emigration
Self-custody status and any registration obligation beyond Form 160 / patrimonio
Foreign-trust / private-foundation recognition for Bitcoin estate planning under forced heirship
How Bitcoin's situs is characterized for succession (lex rei sitae) and Form 160

watchlist.item: Stalled comprehensive digital-assets bill in Congress

product_routing_notes: Colombia users often surface tax_visibility_issue (multi-jurisdiction, FX-registration of crypto inflows) and cross_border_family_gap (forced heirship + heirs/documents across countries). LATAM-fluent positioning is most relevant here.

change_log.change: Initial profile from parallel research pass. Tier-1 DIAN/Banco de la República/SFC sources plus Tier-2 PwC/firm explainers; wealth-tax threshold and exit tax flagged as open questions.
